fund.name: HDFC Flexi Cap Fund - Growth Option - Direct Plan

allocations.rationale: This fund offers broad exposure to large, mid, and small companies, giving your portfolio flexibility. It has delivered consistent 20%+ returns over 3 years with a moderate risk level. As a returning NRI, this reduces your need to time the market.

fund.name: Motilal Oswal Large and Midcap Fund - Direct Plan Growth

allocations.rationale: Focused on fast-growing mid-sized Indian companies, this fund has outperformed peers with 27% 3-year returns. It adds growth potential while staying within large and mid-cap safety.

fund.name: BANDHAN SMALL CAP FUND - DIRECT PLAN GROWTH

allocations.rationale: Small-cap funds can deliver high long-term growth. This top-performing fund has given 31% annual returns over 3 years. We keep allocation limited due to higher volatility.

fund.name: ICICI Prudential Equity & Debt Fund - Direct Plan - Growth

allocations.rationale: This fund mixes stocks and bonds, reducing risk. It's equity-taxed (better for your 30% tax slab) and has delivered 19% returns over 3 years with lower swings than pure stock funds.

fund.name: Motilal Oswal Nifty Midcap 150 Index Fund - Direct Plan

allocations.rationale: This low-cost index fund tracks 150 mid-sized Indian companies. With only 0.2% yearly fee, it ensures you capture market growth without high charges.

fund.name: Sundaram Long Term Tax Advantage Fund Series III Direct Plan - Growth

allocations.rationale: This tax-saving fund qualifies for ₹1.5L/year deduction under Section 80C, saving you up to ₹46,800/year in taxes. It's locked for 3 years but taxed like equity funds, making it ideal for your high tax bracket.

plan.setup_phase:
Step 1: Download Groww, Kuvera, or Coin by Zerodha app.
Step 2: Complete KYC using your PAN and Aadhaar.
Step 3: Link your bank account.
Step 4: Search each fund by name, select 'Direct Plan' and 'Growth'.
Step 5: Start SIPs for all six funds with the amounts shown.
Step 6: Invest your existing ₹45L corpus as lumpsum in the same 40:20:10:20:5:5 ratio across the funds.
review_checkpoints: After 6 months: Check if all SIPs are running and if any fund underperforms its category average.
After 1 year: Review annual returns. If any fund is in bottom 25% of its category, consider replacing it.
Every 3 years: Rebalance portfolio and review tax-saving needs for ELSS.
plan.rebalancing_guidelines: Once a year, check your portfolio mix. If any category (like small cap) grows beyond 15% of total value, sell some and invest in underweight categories to restore the 40-20-10-20-5-5 split. This keeps risk in check.
plan.rationale: At 45, with 17 years to retirement, you can afford moderate risk for higher growth. Your ₹45L corpus and ₹40K/month SIP can grow to ₹5Cr if we average 15.5% returns. The mix balances growth (Flexi, Large & Mid, Small Cap), stability (Aggressive Hybrid), low cost (Index), and tax savings (ELSS). As a returning NRI, this rebuilds your India portfolio systematically.
risks: Stock markets can drop 20-30% in a bad year — your portfolio value will fall temporarily. This is normal and usually recovers in 2-3 years.
Small-cap funds are more volatile — they can fall harder than large funds during market stress.
Your 30% tax slab means debt funds are less efficient; we've avoided them in favor of equity-taxed hybrid and arbitrage-like options.
The ELSS fund locks your money for 3 years — avoid if you need liquidity soon.
plan.disclaimer: For research/educational purposes only. Not certified financial advice.

sip_step_up.description: Increase SIP by 10% every year to match salary growth and accelerate corpus building

aps.reasoning: The investment plan is predominantly active in nature. The equity allocation is largely directed toward actively managed funds (Flexi Cap, Large & Mid Cap, Small Cap, Aggressive Hybrid, ELSS), with only 5% allocated to a passive instrument (Motilal Oswal Nifty Midcap 150 Index Fund). Thus, the passive_instrument_fraction is low at 0.1 (5% of equity allocation in passive). Rebalancing is recommended annually with specific review checkpoints at 6 months, 1 year, and every 3 years, including potential fund replacement based on performance — indicating moderate turnover, hence turnover_score of 0.5. Cost is mentioned in the index fund rationale (0.2% fee highlighted), but not a dominant theme across the portfolio; other fund selections are based on past returns and tax efficiency, so cost_emphasis_score is moderate at 0.6. The plan emphasizes performance and fund-specific outcomes (e.g., '27% 3-year returns', 'outperformed peers'), indicating deep research and active selection, leading to a low research_vs_cost_score of 0.1. The time horizon is long-term (17 years to retirement, 3-year lock-in for ELSS), with compounding and SIP step-up, supporting a high time_horizon_alignment_score of 0.7. Portfolio activeness is high: all selected funds (except the index fund) are actively managed categories with rationales based on outperformance and manager capability; no risk metrics contradict this, so portfolio_activeness_score is 0.1. Overall, the plan leans heavily active across most dimensions.

pqs.reasoning:
The investment plan is well-aligned with Nick Carraway's financial goals, life stage, and 17-year investment horizon. At age 45 with a moderate risk appetite, the 70% equity (Flexi Cap, Large & Mid, Small Cap, Index) and 20% Aggressive Hybrid allocation supports long-term growth toward a ₹5Cr retirement corpus, while the inclusion of Aggressive Hybrid and rebalancing guidelines manages volatility. The plan acknowledges his re-entry into the Indian market after a decade abroad, offering a systematic approach via SIPs and lumpsum deployment of his ₹45L corpus.

Diversification is strong across market caps (large, mid, small), fund types (flexi cap, large & mid, small cap, index, hybrid, ELSS), and investment styles (active and passive). The 5% allocation to a Nifty Midcap 150 index fund adds low-cost, rules-based exposure, enhancing diversification. However, geographic diversification is missing (no international funds despite stated interest), and sector concentration risk isn't addressed, slightly reducing the score.

The risk-return profile is appropriate for a moderate investor: high-growth assets are balanced with risk controls (only 10% in small cap, rebalancing rules, Aggressive Hybrid for stability). Projected returns of 15.5% are optimistic but plausible for an equity-heavy portfolio over 17 years. Downside risks are acknowledged (20-30% drawdowns, small-cap volatility), and the bear case (11.5%) is reasonably conservative.

The plan is internally consistent: the rationale, fund choices, allocation percentages, rebalancing rules, and risk warnings all support a coherent strategy of growth with risk control. The 10% annual SIP step-up aligns with wealth-building over time.

Tax efficiency is excellent. The investor is in the 30% tax slab, and the plan correctly avoids slab-taxed debt/liquid funds. Instead, it uses equity-taxed Aggressive Hybrid (ICICI Equity & Debt) for stability, which is smarter from a tax perspective. It also fully utilizes Section 80C with a 5% ELSS allocation (₹2,000/month = ₹24,000/year, within the ₹1.5L limit), saving up to ₹7,200/year in taxes (30% of ₹24,000). The rationale explicitly justifies ELSS for tax savings and notes the equity tax treatment, showing deep tax awareness. No tax-inefficient instruments are recommended.
